rulings.Case Title: THOMAS DUFFY VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Decision On Its Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 26, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Balliet Bros. Construction Corporation's motion for reconsideration of decision on its motion for summary judgment is denied pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 2.7(B) and 2.11(T) for defendant's failure to provide the Court with a courtesy copy of its memorandum of points and authorities in support of the motion. When considered in conjunction with defendant's two prior failures to provide courtesy copies of papers supporting its motion for summary judgment, this failure warrants the Court to exercise its discretion to deny the motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: VERNON MARION VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Matt Marion As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 26, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Plaintiffs and Petitioner's motion to appoint Matt Marion as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for, deceased plaintiff and for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. The First Amended Complaint is deemed served as of the date of granting this motion on all defendants who have previously appeared. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR HAWKINS ET AL VS. INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 26, 2017 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Milwaukee Valve Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Hawkins was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Hawkins and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Mr. Hawkins' deposition testimony demonstrates that he has knowledge that he worked with gaskets and packing supplied by defendant in replacement kits and plaintiffs' discovery responses demonstrate that plaintiffs possess, or can reasonably obtain, evidence that those parts more likely than not contained asbestos. Additionally, plaintiffs' interrogatory responses identify Mr. Hawkins as having knowledge that gaskets and packing he removed from Milwaukee valves were original and defendant failed to demonstrate that he does not have such knowledge. The Court will not consider defendant's reply evidence. (San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308, Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR HAWKINS ET AL VS. INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, April 26, 2017 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Wismer & Becker Contracting Engineers' motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting the causation element of their claims against defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Hawkins (defendant's Exhibits 1, 2, 3; plaintiffs' Exhibit A) precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Specifically, that testimony demonstrates that Mr. Hawkins has knowledge that he worked in the presence of defendant's employees when they removed insulation from pipes. Whether defendant's employees worked in the presence of Mr. Hawkins is the sole issue raised by defendant's motion. Defendant's objections to the excerpts of Mr. Hawkins' deposition testimony submitted by plaintiffs are overruled. The Court will not consider defendant's reply evidence. (San Diego Watercrafts, Inc. v. Wells Fargo Bank (2002) 102 Cal.App.4th 308, Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
